claim.statement: The hybrid Schur/van der Waerden obstruction is unavoidable for ALL N >= 13 with 3 colors: no 3-coloring of {1..N} avoids both monochromatic Schur triples (x+y=z) and monochromatic 3-term arithmetic progressions, for any N >= 13.
claim.negation_of: an N >= 13 and a 3-coloring of {1..N} that avoids both obstruction families

rung.name: multi-region min-trust
trusted_base: min-trust across regions = the WEAKEST region
region N=13 is R2 (compiled-checker cert); the N>=14 tail rests on an un-formalized monotonicity argument (R5) -- so the whole universal-over-N claim is only as strong as the tail

meta.rule: rung = min-trust over regions; see docs/composition-example.md

meta.format: flat space-separated DIMACS ints consumed by parseCert
meta.cross_reference: same finite claim as hybrid-schur-vdw-3color-le-13 (R3 exhaustive Python recomputation)

encoder.variable_scheme: point-major, zero-based colors, one-based DIMACS variables

checker.promote_recipe: Formalize the restriction/monotonicity lemma: any 3-coloring of {1..N} restricts to a 3-coloring of {1..13}; since the N=13 obstruction is forced (region threshold-N13), every N>=14 case is forced too. As a Lean lemma this region becomes R0/R2 and the whole claim certifies at R2. Until then it is an argued-but-uncertified R5 region.

provenance.pipeline: region N=13 re-checks the real hybrid cert via lratcheck (R2); region N>=14 is a stated monotonicity argument, not yet formalized (R5)

one_directional: A multi-region barrier is exactly as strong as its weakest region. The N=13 region is R2-CERTIFIED, but the N>=14 tail is a manual R5 region with no automated checker, so the composite honestly reports DEFERRED -- the rigorous threshold region cannot launder the universal-over-N claim up to R2. Formalizing the one-line restriction lemma promotes the whole barrier to R2.
